reasoning: Q1 iteration 1 achieved 92.9ms, only 7.5% slower than DuckDB (86.4ms). The dominant bottleneck is scan_filter (56.6ms, 60.9% of total time), which is CPU-bound on arithmetic-heavy operations. SIMD vectorization can reduce scan_filter by 40-50% to 25-30ms, bringing total execution to 60-65ms and surpassing DuckDB. With 1 iteration remaining and a concrete, high-impact optimization path, this final budget should be used aggressively.
focus_areas: SIMD vectorization (AVX2) for date filtering and aggregation arithmetic in hot scan loop
Zone map block pruning to skip predicate evaluation for blocks entirely below/above date cutoff
strategy_notes: Apply SIMD vectorization as the primary optimization (high expected impact: 40-50% reduction in scan_filter). The scan_filter loop (lines 204-241 in q1.cpp) processes 59.9M rows with scalar floating-point operations. Vectorize using AVX2 (_mm256_load_pd, _mm256_mul_pd, _mm256_sub_pd) to process 4 doubles per instruction, reducing scan_filter from 56.6ms to ~25-30ms. Key tactics: (1) Load 4 shipdate values with _mm256_loadu_si256, compare against cutoff with _mm256_cmpgt_epi32 for selection mask. (2) Vectorize aggregation arithmetic (disc_price and charge computations) using _mm256_mul_pd, _mm256_sub_pd. (3) Perform horizontal reduction with _mm256_hadd_pd at morsel boundaries. (4) Use _mm256_maskstore_pd for branch-free conditional updates. Secondary optimization: implement zone map block pruning to eliminate predicate evaluation overhead for blocks entirely below/above the date threshold. The lineitem table is sorted by l_shipdate (100K row blocks, ~600 blocks total), so blocks can be skipped entirely if their max_shipdate < cutoff_date (10472). Expected combined impact: 60-65ms total execution time, a 35% improvement over baseline and surpassing DuckDB.
notes: This is the final iteration. SIMD is a proven, high-impact optimization with medium risk (vectorization complexity, but well-understood technique). If SIMD intrinsics cause compilation issues, the fallback is loop unrolling with compiler pragmas (priority 6), which can achieve 20-30% improvement. Zone maps are low-risk and complement SIMD by reducing predicate evaluation overhead.
